core_mechanism: a spontaneous ordering transition of coupled continuous-phase (rotor) variables in which the existence and critical threshold of the ordered state are set by the competition between long-wavelength Goldstone-mode (spinwave) fluctuations and topological-defect (vortex) proliferation, with the connectivity/curvature of the underlying substrate determining whether order can survive
brief_reason: The abstract locates the transition of a continuous-phase (XY/rotor) system via Binder's cumulant and attributes the absence of a finite-temperature transition on the negatively curved lattice to strong spinwave-type fluctuations analyzed alongside vortex density, i.e. substrate geometry tuning the fluctuation-vs-defect balance that controls continuous-symmetry ordering.
